stocks.recent_news: Bank of Maharashtra Q4 FY26 results 20-Apr-2026: Net profit Rs 2,014 cr (+34.9% YoY from Rs 1,493 cr); FY26 PAT record Rs 7,019 cr (+27.2% YoY). NII Rs 3,702 cr (+18.8% YoY). Advances +22% YoY to Rs 2.92 tn, deposits +14% YoY to Rs 3.51 tn, total business +17% YoY. GNPA improved to 1.45% (from 1.74%), NNPA to 0.13% (from 0.18%). CASA 52.51% sector-leading. CAR 18.36%, CET1 14.59%. Final dividend Rs 1.20/share proposed (on top of Rs 1 interim). Stock hit fresh 52-week high on 21-Apr near Rs 80. HDFC Securities retained BUY with TP Rs 90 (1.5x Mar-2028 ABVPS).
stocks.sector_context: Nifty PSU Bank index up ~15% CYTD 2026 vs Nifty 50 +9.6% - leading sectoral performer. Index P/E ~8.73 considered fairly valued; 52wk high 9,918.65 (26-Feb-2026). Strong Q4 business updates from PNB, Union Bank, BoM drove index +2%+. Tailwinds: RBI rate-cut cycle, healthy credit growth. Headwinds: NIM compression as deposit repricing lags rate cuts; PSBs expected to maintain rather than expand spreads.
stocks.peer_comparison: MAHABANK best-in-class within PSU pack: 22% advance growth at top end (Central Bank 18.9%), NNPA 0.13% sector-best, CASA 52.51% sector-leading, PAT growth +35% YoY beats larger PSU peers (SBI, BoB). Smaller balance sheet than SBI/BoB/Canara but superior return ratios. Valuation discount vs larger PSBs sustains re-rating potential.
red_flags: Fresh 52-week high post-results - near-term upside partly priced in
NIM compression risk as deposit repricing lags RBI rate cuts
Mid-cap PSB liquidity premium vs larger peers
Geopolitical overhang on sustained PSB rally
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 1.20/share record date pending
FY27 business growth guidance from management
Analyst target upgrades post strong Q4 print (HDFC Sec already at Rs 90 BUY)
Continued PSU Bank index outperformance

stocks.recent_news: PNB Housing Q4/FY26 results 20-Apr-2026: Q4 PAT +19% YoY; FY26 net profit record Rs 2,291 cr (+18.3% YoY), EPS Rs 87.94 (+20.3%). Loan assets Rs 87,347 cr (+15.3%), disbursements Rs 26,548 cr (+20.8%). GNPA dropped below 1% for first time to 0.93% (from ~1.5%). NII Rs 3,110 cr (+13.1%). Final dividend Rs 8/share. Stock +8.92% on 21-Apr to ~Rs 987.65; opened Rs 949.50 and touched Rs 1,007. Morgan Stanley TP Rs 1,160; Motilal Oswal Rs 1,200; ICICI Securities upgraded to BUY with TP raised to Rs 1,350 (from Rs 990). MarketsMojo upgraded quality grade.
stocks.sector_context: India housing finance sector growing ~16% p.a.; home loan market USD 430.74 bn in 2026, projected USD 809 bn by 2031 (13.44% CAGR). RBI rate-cut cycle supportive of disbursements. PSU banks hold 47.33% market share; NBFCs growing faster via self-employed underwriting. Morgan Stanley warns of consensus EPS cuts for broader NBFC space, urging selective approach.
stocks.peer_comparison: PNBHOUSING trailing P/E ~10-11x - materially cheaper than Bajaj Housing (~30x), premium to LIC HFL (~5-6x). GNPA 0.93% materially better than most HFCs. PAT growth +19% ahead of sector average 13-16%. Strongest near-term re-rating candidate given multiple analyst upgrades within 48 hours of print.
red_flags: Post-result rally ~9% already digested
GNPA at cyclical low (0.93%) likely to mean-revert higher
Historical PNB promoter transition overhang lingers in narrative
Sector EPS-cut warning from Morgan Stanley on NBFCs broadly
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 8/share record date
Analyst TP upgrade cycle continues (ICICI Sec Rs 1,350 latest)
AUM growth on housing-finance tailwinds
RBI rate cuts transmitting to HFC disbursement growth

stocks.recent_news: Nippon India Gold BeES at Rs 124.99 on 22-Apr-2026; 52W range Rs 76.36-148.14 (currently ~16% off high). AUM Rs 54,127.54 cr, expense ratio 0.80%. Tracks Indian physical gold; 24K gold at Rs 15,475/gram on 23-Apr. Gold had weakest monthly USD performance in 13 years in March but recovered in April (net inflows Rs 17.36 bn during 1-10 Apr). COMEX spot $4,752/oz (22-Apr), +7.82% MoM, +43.32% YoY. MCX gold ~Rs 1.52 lakh/10g, +2% WoW.
stocks.sector_context: Goldman Sachs long-term bullish - $5,400/oz target by late 2026 (~Rs 1.7-1.9 lakh/10g). JP Morgan forecasts $5,055/oz by Q4 2026. Drivers: INR at record low, inflation, geopolitical tension (Middle East / Strait of Hormuz), crypto outflows into bullion, central bank gold buying. Headwind: US-Iran ceasefire easing premium; post-Akshaya Tritiya physical demand moderating. Next-week forecasts lean bullish (~+3% to $4,924).
stocks.peer_comparison: GOLDBEES is India's largest and most liquid gold ETF (AUM Rs 54,128 cr) - clear category leader ahead of SETFGOLD, KOTAKGOLD, HDFCGOLD, SBIGETS. 0.80% expense ratio mid-pack. Minimal tracking error given scale.
red_flags: Price ~16% below 52-week high - momentum weakened from peak
US-Iran ceasefire removes some geopolitical premium
Profit-booking after March-April volatility and 13-year-worst USD drop in March
Post-Akshaya Tritiya physical demand cooling
Pure commodity exposure - no company-specific catalysts
catalysts: Goldman Sachs / JPM bullish long-term targets ($5,055-5,400/oz)
INR weakness lifting rupee-denominated gold
Summer wedding season retail demand in India
Central bank gold buying continuing globally
Geopolitical re-escalation (Middle East, Ukraine) trigger

stocks.recent_news: CCI approved Torrent Power's 100% acquisition of Nabha Power from L&T on 07-Apr-2026; EV Rs 6,889 cr, equity/convertible Rs 3,660.87 cr; closure by 30-Jun-2026. Nabha is 2x700 MW supercritical thermal at Rajpura, Punjab (FY25: Rs 4,866 cr revenue, Rs 1,153 cr adj. EBITDA, 95.36% PAF). Post-deal operational capacity 5 GW -> 6.4 GW. Q3 FY26 net profit +35.2% YoY to Rs 643 cr. Management met Kotak IE in Ahmedabad 13-Apr-2026. Stock ~Rs 1,621 on 21-Apr (52W range Rs 1,188-1,637.6 - within 1% of high). Q4 FY26 results due May 2026; analyst estimates: revenue Rs 5,000-5,400 cr, PAT Rs 560-630 cr. Expected FY26 dividend Rs 15/share.
stocks.sector_context: Indian power sector strongly outperforming - NTPC/Adani Power/Tata Power/Power Grid up to 30% CYTD 2026; Adani Green +37%, Adani Power +24% in April alone. IMD projects above-normal temperatures and more heatwave days March-May 2026; peak demand forecast 275-285 GW. JM Financial top summer picks: Adani Power, Adani Green, Tata Power, Torrent Power, JSW Energy. PL Capital: NTPC TP Rs 423, Tata Power Rs 359. Consensus Torrent 12M TP Rs 2,000-2,300 (19-37% upside).
stocks.peer_comparison: Analyst preference tilted to Tata Power, NTPC, Adani Power for summer-demand plays over TORNTPOWER. Torrent's Nabha acquisition adds thermal when peers pivot renewables - differentiated but off-theme. P/E 25.03, P/B 4.34 - rich for regulated utility. Q3 FY26 PAT growth +35.2% in line with top-tier peers. Weight in Nifty Energy 1.27%.
red_flags: Rs 6,889 cr Nabha acquisition funding and integration risk
Thermal-heavy expansion (+1.4 GW coal) vs peer renewable pivots
Trading within 1% of 52-week high - limited margin of safety
P/B 4.34 rich for regulated utility business
Punjab discom receivables risk on Nabha
Q4 FY26 consensus is a ~5% top-line growth only (seasonally weak transmission)
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results in May 2026 and board dividend recommendation (Rs 15 expected)
Nabha Power deal closure by 30-Jun-2026; FY27 consolidation
Summer peak electricity demand 275-285 GW boosting merchant rates
IMD hotter-than-normal summer forecast

stocks.recent_news: Nestle India Q4 FY26 (released 21-Apr-2026): Net profit Rs 1,110.9 cr (+27.2% YoY; beat consensus by 28.9%); revenue Rs 6,747.8 cr (+22.6% YoY from Rs 5,503.88 cr; beat by 7.9%) - highest-ever domestic sales Rs 6,445 cr. EBITDA margin 26.3% despite >50% YoY A&P spend increase. Double-digit volume growth (~18%). Final dividend Rs 5/share declared; total FY26 dividend Rs 12 (incl. Rs 7 interim paid Jan-2026). AGM 3-Jul-2026; record date 10-Jul-2026; payout on/after 30-Jul-2026. Stock surged +8.43% to Rs 1,394.90 on 21-Apr (fresh 52W high), extended ~3% on 22-Apr to ~Rs 1,421.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty FMCG at 51,404 (22-Apr), -6% CYTD 2026 vs Nifty 50 -0.8% - sector laggard, but recent defensive rotation drove +2.55% led by Nestle, HUL, Varun Beverages. Sector expected to reach Rs 10.2 lakh cr by 2026 at 9-11% CAGR; urban demand soft, input-cost pressure (crude, palm, cocoa, coffee). HUL board meets 30-Apr for Q4; HUL Q3 FY26 revenue +5.7% / UVG 4% - margin over volume guidance. Shrinkflation widespread.
stocks.peer_comparison: Nestle clear outperformer in FMCG - ~18% volume growth vs HUL ~4% UVG and sector ~2.6%. P/E 71.7-80.2x richest in sector (Britannia 55-57x, HUL 33-53x). Premium supported by brand moat (Maggi, KitKat, Nescafe) and growth delivery; quality-premium trade intact. ITC/HUL consensus 12M upside ~31% vs Nestle's smaller implied upside post-rally.
red_flags: Stretched valuation at P/E ~72-80x vs sector range 33-58x
Fresh 52-week high post-results - sell-on-news risk
A&P spend +50% YoY could compress margins if volume moderates
Sector-wide urban demand weakness a drag if prolonged
Q4 beat fully priced - high bar for near-term outperformance
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 5/share record date 10-Jul-2026; AGM 3-Jul-2026; pay 30-Jul-2026
Continued volume-led market share gains vs weak peers
Input-cost moderation in H2 2026 (cocoa, coffee, palm)
Defensive rotation trade if broader market stays choppy
HUL/Britannia/ITC Q4 results (late Apr-May) read-through
